review_notes:
- Confirm the benchmark launcher runs from the staged goal input root, or explicitly `cd` there before invoking `lmp`; the input scripts use `read_data water_216_data.lmp` as a relative path.
- Confirm that `bond_seconds` is interpreted as the stock LAMMPS `Bond` timing section. It aggregates bond, angle, dihedral, and improper work; for this water deck only bond and angle are present, so it is a valid proxy here.
- Prefer reporting throughput in timesteps/s (or atom-step/s) rather than ns/day, because this workload uses `units electron` and time-unit conversions are less standard than in `real` or `metal`.
- Keep `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1` and avoid `-sf omp` / `-pk omp` in the benchmark command unless you intentionally want `/omp` variants instead of the base `bond_class2.cpp` and `angle_harmonic.cpp` kernels.
- The tolerance values in the JSON are workflow-level suggestions. The existing style unit tests are much tighter, but practical end-to-end TIP4P NVE tolerances should be calibrated once a baseline run is available.
- `build/lmp` was not present during validation in this workspace. The benchmark validates structurally, but authoritative runs depend on `runtime.pre_commands` succeeding and producing that binary.
- The objective uses weighted median `bond_seconds`; I downweighted the held-out validation cases (`test-32r-long` at `0.5`, `test-64r-short` at `0.25`) so the train cases dominate optimization direction while the test cases still matter.
- Correctness is enforced with thermo series, NVE drift, and a final-state sampled force dump. The optional direct `bond_pe_sum` and `angle_pe_sum` quantities are not emitted by this runner.
- The runner expects staged workload files under `.fermilink-optimize/inputs/all` unless `FERMILINK_GOAL_INPUT_ROOT` overrides it, and it expects standard LAMMPS `timer full` output with `Bond`, `Pair`, `Kspace`, `Neigh`, and `Comm` rows.
